title: The Depth of the Category of Categories

analysis.object: T697: The Depth of the Category of Categories depth analysis

cat_classification.description: Cat is classification system for categories; d(classification) = 3 (T246 analog)
cat_classification.reason: Cat as classifier: d(Cat) = 3 because Cat is the classification system for categories; T246 applies

contains_emlinf.description: Cat contains inf-category objects (EML-inf); does containment imply depth?
contains_emlinf.reason: Cat contains EML-inf objects but is a classifier; classifier depth = 3 regardless of contained depth

resolution.description: Resolution: d(Cat) = 3 because Cat classifies; the EML-inf objects within Cat are classified at depth 3 from Cat's perspective
resolution.reason: Cat depth theorem: d(Cat) = 3; Cat observes EML-inf from EML-3; this is the meta-depth of classification

five_theorems.description: This single question generates five theorems depending on containment vs classification distinction
five_theorems.reason: Category of categories generates 5 theorems: containment, classification, self-reference, EML-inf objects, meta-depth

analysis.theorem: T697: The Depth of the Category of Categories (S976).

key_theorem: T697: The Depth of the Category of Categories (S976).
